results.title: US Congress unlocks US engine sale for Turkey’s KAAN fighter
results.description: US Congress unlocks US engine sale for Turkey’s KAAN fighter For Turkey, the congressional review marks an important milestone for the KAAN programme, which Ankara sees as central to reducing reliance on foreign combat aircraft. Saturday 11/07/2026 A view of a KAAN fighter jet during runway tests in Ankara. WASHINGTON A major congressional hurdle has been removed from the planned US sale of F110 fighter engines for Turkey’s indigenous KAAN combat aircraft after lawmakers failed to advance legislation blocking the deal, allowing Ankara’s flagship aerospace programme to move into its next phase. The 15-day congressional review period expired on July 9 without either chamber taking up a joint resolution introduced by nine Democratic lawmakers seeking to halt the transfer of engines and related defence services. With no legislative action taken before the deadline, the proposed sale can now proceed under US arms export procedures. The State Department formally notified Congress of the transaction on June 24, activating the expedited review process that applies to NATO allies. Although members of Congress retain the right to challenge proposed foreign military sales, blocking such deals requires passage of a joint resolution by both the House of Representatives and the Senate, followed by presidential approval or, in the event of a veto, a two-thirds majority in both chambers. The resolution never reached the floor of either chamber, effectively clearing one of the final political obstacles facing the engine package destined for Turkey’s fifth-generation KAAN fighter programme. The proposed sale covers the integration, installation and certification of F110-GE-129E/F engines, together with testing, technical assistance, defence services and the transfer of associated technical data required to support the aircraft’s development. The unsuccessful effort to stop the deal was led by Democratic Representative Dina Titus of Nevada and supported by eight Democratic colleagues, including Brad Sherman, Chris Pappas and Jim McGovern, lawmakers who have consistently opposed previous US defence sales to Turkey, including F-16 fighter aircraft. Other supporters of the measure, Jim Costa, Josh Gottheimer, Mike Quigley and George Latimer, represent districts with influential Armenian, Greek and Israeli communities that have traditionally lobbied against military exports to Ankara. The latest development comes amid signs of a broader thaw in US-Turkish defence relations. Earlier this week, the New York Times , citing four senior US administration officials, reported that President Donald Trump was expected to tell Turkish President Recep Tayyip Erdogan he was prepared to allow Turkey to re-enter the F-35 stealth fighter programme, from which Ankara was removed in 2019. For Turkey, the congressional review marks an important milestone for the KAAN programme, which Ankara sees as central to reducing reliance on foreign combat aircraft while strengthening its domestic defence industry. Although the expiration of the review period does not conclude the procurement process, it removes the immediate risk of congressional intervention that could have delayed or derailed the project. Attention will now shift to detailed negotiations involving the US administration, the engine manufacturer and Turkish officials over delivery schedules, technical integration, certification and flight-testing, steps regarded as essential before the engines can be incorporated into Turkey’s next-generation fighter aircraft.

results.title: الصين.. إجلاء أكثر من 600 ألف شخص مع اقتراب الإعصار "بافي"
results.description:
أجلت السلطات الصينية اليوم أكثر من 600 ألف شخص في إقليمي تشجيانغ وفوجيان، مع اقتراب الإعصار "بافي" من سواحل شرق الصين، حيث يُتوقع أن يصل إلى اليابسة قرب مدينة ونتشو خلال الساعات الأولى من غدٍ الأحد.

وأفادت وسائل إعلام رسمية بأن عمليات الإجلاء شملت أكثر من نصف مليون شخص في إقليم تشجيانغ، إضافة إلى أكثر من 100 ألف شخص في إقليم فوجيان، في إطار الإجراءات الاحترازية لمواجهة الإعصار الذي لا يزال يشكل خطرًا بسبب الأمطار الغزيرة التي يصاحبها، رغم تراجع شدته تدريجيًا.
